content.text:
I have successfully completed the bug fix. Here's a summary of what I did:

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in the `addAll(int index, Collection coll)` method in `SetUniqueList.java`. The original implementation was ignoring the `index` parameter and always adding elements to the end of the list using the `add(Object)` method, rather than inserting them at the specified index while maintaining uniqueness.

## Solution
I modified the `addAll(int index, Collection coll)` method to:
1. Iterate through the collection elements individually
2. Check if each element already exists in the list using `set.contains(obj)`
3. If the element is unique, add it at the specified index using `super.add(index, obj)`
4. Add the element to the tracking set
5. Increment the index so that the next element gets inserted at the correct position

## Key Changes
- Instead of calling `add(it.next())` (which adds to the end of the list), I added logic to check for uniqueness first with `set.contains(obj) == false`
- Used `super.add(index, obj)` to insert at the correct position when the element is unique
- Incremented the index after each successful insertion to account for the new element's position
- Maintained the set tracking for uniqueness

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.apache.commons.collections.list.TestSetUniqueList::testIntCollectionAddAll` now passes
- Related tests continue to pass, ensuring no regression was introduced
- The code compiles correctly

The fix properly handles the insertion of unique elements at a specified index while maintaining the semantics of the SetUniqueList class.

result:
I have successfully completed the bug fix. Here's a summary of what I did:

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in the `addAll(int index, Collection coll)` method in `SetUniqueList.java`. The original implementation was ignoring the `index` parameter and always adding elements to the end of the list using the `add(Object)` method, rather than inserting them at the specified index while maintaining uniqueness.

## Solution
I modified the `addAll(int index, Collection coll)` method to:
1. Iterate through the collection elements individually
2. Check if each element already exists in the list using `set.contains(obj)`
3. If the element is unique, add it at the specified index using `super.add(index, obj)`
4. Add the element to the tracking set
5. Increment the index so that the next element gets inserted at the correct position

## Key Changes
- Instead of calling `add(it.next())` (which adds to the end of the list), I added logic to check for uniqueness first with `set.contains(obj) == false`
- Used `super.add(index, obj)` to insert at the correct position when the element is unique
- Incremented the index after each successful insertion to account for the new element's position
- Maintained the set tracking for uniqueness

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.apache.commons.collections.list.TestSetUniqueList::testIntCollectionAddAll` now passes
- Related tests continue to pass, ensuring no regression was introduced
- The code compiles correctly

The fix properly handles the insertion of unique elements at a specified index while maintaining the semantics of the SetUniqueList class.
